reviews.review_text: Leave what must be left behind exactly where it belongs, lest you wander endlessly through a fog of your own making, forever searching for your own executioner.

I still can't believe a human actually created this- absolute genius. this game turned fear into art.
Bro i've never felt this kind of creepy feeling while playing in my life, this game is just different; the sounds, the music, the atmosphere, the monsters... every damn thing; it has this weird vibe that no other game has.
The story writing is next level and the attention to detail in this game is insane. Everything feels like it has a purpose.
A true psychological horror- absolute cinema 10/10.

Silent Hill 2 is straight up insane.

The story is easily the best narrative I’ve ever experienced in a horror game. The plot twists are absolutely wild and even the side characters like Eddie, Laura, Angela and Maria all have so much depth and tragic backstories. Angela’s story especially hit me really hard. The atmosphere in this game is by far the best horror atmosphere I’ve ever felt. It’s pure dread and sadness mixed together in the most beautiful way.
The amount of insane details everywhere is crazy. Every corner, every sound, every little thing feels so carefully made. The graphics look beautiful and the locations are incredibly atmospheric.
The puzzles are cool and varied, and the combat is actually pretty fun too.

Of course it’s not perfect. The performance can be rough sometimes. I had a few crashes and random FPS drops. There’s also way too much enemy spam. I liked the combat, but I would’ve preferred fewer but stronger enemies instead of getting swarmed all the time. Some sections also felt a bit too long and stretched out.
Still, even with those problems, this game is absolutely crazy good. On Standard it was a bit too easy for me, but overall it’s one of the strongest experiences I’ve had in years. If you like psychological horror, this is a must-play. Highly recommended.

9/10

Possibly the greatest horror ever made. The game is scary af , very unsettling and depressing. I don't think I ever played something as dark and scary as this. 

When I was a kid there was a game called The Suffering it was very scary and since then there were no other games that scared me as much as The Suffering did. But Silent Hill 2 even tops it. 
Also scaring a grown man is harder than a little kid but SH2 manages to do so. 

And by scary I don't mean cheap screamers, the game barely has any. It scares you with the darkness and the atmosphere. It is so dark that half of the time your brain is imagining things that aren't even there and it is the scariest thing ever because in your imagination everything feels super real. No amount of polygons and ray tracing can create level of reality that your brain does in your head. The scariest movies and games barely show you any monsters, they let yout brain imagine them and it is much scarier than just another CGI zombie on the screen.

Peak horror experience and masterpiece.

ended up beating this on PS5, GREAT GAME LOVE IT!. but classic ♥♥♥♥ ASS PC OPTIMIZATION, genuinely thought my pc was gonna light up playing this. game devs need to start caring about optimizing games again

James walks the fog like a penitent carrying his own coffin. Mary’s letter is the noose; the town tightens it. Pyramid Head is not a monster—he is the verdict you wrote before you arrived. Every corridor a confessional, every mannequin a mirror that learned to hate you.

Combat is clumsy grace, survival-horror liturgy: swing, bleed, pray the bullets last. Puzzles are occult riddles carved into flesh and rust. The remake polishes the blade without dulling the guilt—Bloober knelt at the altar and did not flinch. Multiple endings wait like hanging judges.

Atmosphere so thick it clots the lungs. Sound design that whispers your sins back at you. A masterpiece of wretched beauty.

The fog knows your name. Enter anyway.
very atmospheric and tension building with creepy ahh creature designs, and i know its a puzzle game but... theres just too many damn puzzles.
If you like resident evil style games, you will enjoy this. Lots of puzzles, monsters, and nice storyline
A masterpiece that had me on my toes the whole time. However, some sections of the game were a bit bloated but I enjoyed it wholeheartedly.

I've played both the original and this remake. The remake is worth your time and I found it even more enjoyable and scarier than the original. Team Bloober did an excellent job. That said, the game is marred by performance issues (stuttering and game crashes) irrespective of hardware. Were it not for that, I'd say this would be the definitive edition of the game.

Silent Hill 2 is pure dark psychological horror done right.
It’s not about jump scares — it’s about guilt, trauma, and the kind of silence that feels heavier than noise. The atmosphere, sound design, and storytelling make everything feel deeply unsettling in the best way.

A haunting experience that stays with you long after you stop playing.

I played this game multiple times, on multiple platforms, and i will do it again to 100% achievements on my main account, i just love it ,every single detail is so great .but the frames sometimes drop:(

This is a largely faithful adaptation to the original material, in terms of story tone and character behavior as well as the lore documents you find. The combat can be a bit of a slog but that's never really been the strong point of this franchise. Admittedly, certain sections of the game seem to drag on for longer than necessary, but that is a minor flaw in an otherwise excellent re-imagining of a cornerstone of videogame horror media. It is absolutely worth your time if you have any love for the series. Congratulations to bloober team for knocking this (almost) entirely out of the park.

its a good remake sure, better than most remakes and remasters being released today, but its just too stretched out to the point its not even fun sometimes, just doing too much to make the game longer, original was 4-5 hours long while this is 11-12 hours long, combat can get repetitive so fast and the modern gameplay sure is more accessible but just makes the game way less unique and basic, feels like resident evil 2 remake, voice acting is also more basic and sounds like a netflix dub, its still a good game nonetheless, good for people who just cant stand tank controls because they're too hard and confusing!!!!! (honestly dude just grow up), i played original and remake back to back and i didn't grow up with ps2 games so this isn't nostalgia bias, oh and this game runs like poopoo too

overall i liked the experience, the only downside that it does felt like they were dragging it just for a sake of the gamelength. also i would prefer them to remake it whole without cut-out scenes, you met a lot of memoria for those during your exploration.
Phenomenal game and a great example of what the medium can offer. Superb story, gameplay, visuals, MUSIC, and all wrapped around psychoanalysis. This game will stay with you long after you are done.

It was my first Silent Hill ever and honestly… probably the best horror I’ve played.

The game keeps throwing surprises and scares at you the whole time. Combat is pretty easy and kinda repetitive tho - dodge, hit, dodge, hit.
But the visuals and overall audio/atmosphere? absolutely insane, masterpiece vibe.

Also the puzzles you have to solve to get items and unlock new paths? really well thought out, great design.

This game is something, No other game has made me so uncomfortable. The world is like nothing else, the existential dread that you feel from every minute, every character, Everything in Silent Hill. 

I would recommend this to anyone looking for something to make them uncomfortable, for something to make you scared, for something to make you cry. 

i fully recommend this game to anyone with the palate for it.

My favourite game of all time, I love how well they did with this remake. It resonates with me in ways i'll never truly understand. Everything about it is beautiful. The story, the depth, the aesthetic. I dream of this place.

Innovative But Repetitive

Overall, it has better gameplay than most survival horror games. Unfortunately, it’s about 4 times as long as it needs to be. It’s unique in that it has a more abstract and psychological horror theme.

I hadn’t played the original. I only did a casual playthrough. There were definitely some tense parts.

The audio was good. Most of the music is not noticeable. I liked that the enemy noises were spatial. I could locate right or left based on the stereo headphones. I wish the radio that indicated nearby enemies worked like the sound in Zelda Tears of the Kingdom. In that game, sound bounces on walls and needs to reach the player in order to be heard. In this game, there is some bleeding in nearby rooms which is distracting. Some of the tenser parts were just audio recordings played in certain areas.

My main issue is with the length. There is a lot of repetition. The game takes about 15 hours. There are only about 5 common enemies. The bosses are all unique, which is appreciated. The levels run together. The levels have too many repeated assets. There is a kinda backrooms feel which is good, but a lot of the rooms start to look the same early in the game. They reuse the main character jumping into bottomless pits or putting his hand where he shouldn’t way too many times.

Generally, the visuals are good. The characters and the levels are photo realistic. The enemies are abstract. I like the way the little bugs swarm. Some of the visual magic was lost when I saw the bugs despawn. Also, the leaves and the debris seemed to spawn and despawn randomly in the beginning. It was distracting.

The game isn’t well optimized. I only have a Nvidia GTX 2070. The framerate went into the single digits a few times.

This review is very critical. The no here means that I wouldn’t recommend going out of your way to play it.
Bloober Team really captured dissociation, despair, and pure terror with this remake. Absolutely love it!
Because of Unreal Engine 5, its Really Badly optimised, still the best horror game. Highly Recomended.
Truly an experience that i would recommend to any gamer, genuinely scary game with so many layers of symbolism and psychology behind every scene and character in game. 10/10

Best Atmospheric Horror game ever made. 

Combat could be mildly improved by adding a parry system or something, that way you can feel like you're improving rather than waiting for your turn but its fun for the most part. 

I have never felt this emotional after a game other than RDR2. Be patient, this is a pretty thought provoking game so if you're expecting RE:4 levels of action movie nonsense go back to weeny hutt jrs. This is one for the adults. Go play Fortnite Timmy. 

LOVED THIS GAME, way more than I thought I ever would. Pick this one up! 

9/10

very spooky. I usually play games on normal for everything but im a ♥♥♥♥♥ so i played it on light combat. So far super comfortable! My fear of everything is less hindered by some possible frustration of combat. I recommend the game is super cool even though im only like halfway through.

Silent Hill? I can't even remember what happenend. All I know was this game was far beyond any other video game I have ever played in my life. Everything about the game was perfection, the storytelling, the puzzles, the characters, it was absolute cinema to me.

Silent Hill Two is really good, so of course the remake of it is going to be good. That being said, the three nitpicks I had with the game Are as follows. They cut out Toluca Lake. You don't even get the chance to wield the Pyramid Head Sword. And Some of the new places that they added don't really do it for me, but the game is still good. The story is still the same. They didn't defile the corpse of Silent Hill like I kind of expected them to. I recommend it. I only wish that they added DLC to play. The chapter for Maria. That was Xbox exclusive.

A 10/10 psychological horror masterpiece that I can't stop thinking about, and the story gave me more topics for me to talk about in my next therapy sessions. 

This game is so amazing that it ruined Silent Hill F for me. I made the mistake of trying to play Silent Hill F immediately after finishing Silent Hill 2, and Silent Hill F felt underwhelming and a letdown to me.

Трудно что то сказать по поводу этой игры все и так всё знают. Хороший хорор который пугает атмосферой а не скримерами (хотя они тут тоже есть). Я бы Сайлент Хилл внес в "пантеон" игр которые нужно поиграть хотя-бы раз в жизни. Очень цепляющий сюжет и атмосфера которою нужно прочуствовать всем! Однозначно рекомендую пройти

This game holds a special place in my heart, the remake will always be a classic but there is just something to this remake that just makes it so good. I loved the story, the gameplay and just james himself.

He's just like me fr

drinking random "health drinks" he finds 
injecting random needles laying around with god knows what into him
Overwhelming Guilt and longing

byeah i really liked this game but had to tweak a bitof stuff to make it run nicer at first (especially cutscenes)

Its a really good adaptation of silent hill 2. I think the og is a better experience. Def play the enhanced edition if you have not played it . Requires a pretty insane pc to run it without stuttering so unless your okay with performance issues i would suggest try the console version or expect to spend alot of time messing with settings to get this thing to run right if your on anything less then a 6700xt and a i5 or better. Trust me i love the game and have tried running it on everything from handhelds to egpu's ect. I really wish they could optimize this game better. Its so heavy.. its prolly the worst unreal 5 optimized game and thats saying alot. lol.... it looks amazing but man at what cost. Its a shame because it would be great otherwise.

I've never played the original (or any SH game for that matter), so there is no nostalgia and no comparison. I am rating this game on its own. And I think it is a true (albeit a bit too long) masterpiece.
I've never before engaged so deeply and obsessively with a game's themes, motifs and mysteries. I've never felt so compelled to understand the story. I've never felt such drive to sit with discomfort and fear for many hours. Silent Hill 2 captivated me from the start and never let go. And after I've finished and thought about it for a few days, I am convinced it's one of the most mature and deep pieces of media I've encountered.
A fair warning: if you want a straightforward horror game with focus on mechanics and quick pacing, you might want to stay clear. This game is exhausting at times, rather long, the combat is (presumably on purpose) somewhat clunky and there is so much backtracking that you'll probably know each location pretty much by heart. There are a couple of frustrating puzzles, but in general I've found the difficulty just right to let me progress constantly while feeling clever.
If you like atmosphere, mystery, complex characters and deep immersion, you will love this game. If you're willing to give it your full attention and engage with it the way it asks you to, it is SO incredibly worth it. Go and play it, let it consume you for a while!
It's a good updating for a classic game. Played the original on the PS and it was one of the best games ever. This version is just as good and plays perfectly on Linux via Proton. Recommended!

I really liked this remake. That being said, lets start with what I don't like about this game

its a lot longer than the original, can make certain areas feel like they're dragging on for a long amount of time. If you want to replay for more endings, even after knowing how to get through each area efficiently it will still take you a minimum of 7-9 hours.

those ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ manaquin leg things, they are ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ everywhere and they do not trigger the radio. they are frightening especially when they catch you off guard but in my experience also really annoying.

not being able to slide down ladders. this becomes annoying in the labyrinth during a specific part, it's annoying that james is alg to yeet himself into a pitch black pit repeatly but he doesn't slide down ladders 

Thats it, that's the only thing I don't like about this remake. 

everything else is amazing, The music is spectacular as you'd expect. atmosphere is great, reworked boss fights are great, dodge mechanic is a welcome addition. I really like how although they have changed lots about how this game plays out in terms of locations and puzzles that the way the original played out isn't forgotten. it's actively acknowleged through "glimpses" that almost imply that the original game is 100% canon and has happened or is happening simaltenously to this? (I don't know what it implies, but it's implying something). 
game ran really well for me, I only crashed one time after an extended session and it happened shortly after saving so it was of no real consequence.
The best horror game to date. It hit me so hard that I felt depressed for a week afterward.

The Silent Hill 2 remake brilliantly honors the classic original, which I also deeply loved, while masterfully elevating the psychological horror genre for a modern audience. The game's thick, suffocating atmosphere and unsettling sound design create an incredibly immersive, deeply disturbing sense of dread. At its core, the narrative feels heavily inspired by Dostoevsky’s The Crime and Punishment, exploring profound guilt and courageously tackling taboo themes rarely discussed in media, such as the agonizing burden of terminal illness, sexual trauma, and the dark psychological coping mechanisms of extreme grief. The expanded boss battles are an incredible adrenaline rush compared to the original and while everyone's experience varies, the claustrophobic showdown against Eddie personally felt like the most brutal, nerve-wracking challenge of my entire playthrough.

this game is a master piece in atmosphere and psychological story telling. every detail is on purpose and there are layers to everything. so much is said without being said and written between the lines and on the walls. great game

An unsettling and depressing master piece. 
the sounds, the music, the atmosphere, the monsters; this game is like no other horror game it is just truly a phenomenal game. 
everything in this game has been made with care, everything is meant to make u feel fear and anxiety a beautifully written story with no inconsistency's.  
a psychological master piece - 10/10
Very unique survival horror game, atmosphere like no other. even coming back to it later it's more optimized graphically. 9/10

"For me, it's always like this."
The story, game atmosphere, sounds, music and hidden details are done incredibly well.

This is a spooky game, pretty good first play through in 2026. only negative is that I have a 3080 and ray tracing kept nuking my frame rate, never had this issue with any other game. Once I figured out that is what it was I was able to enjoy the game for what it is. Puzzles are not super obvious and i love that. Its challenging without being tooo tooo hard. Never have played another SH game before so this was a great intro given what I have seen over the years. Excited to see where the story goes! 10 hours in at time of review~

AMAZING GAME. This is my all time favorite horror game, but there is one huge issue:  Optimization.
To fix this game and make it even remotely playable on most PCs, even higher end ones, you need to add -dx11 to the game launch options.  This sounds complicated, but it's not, at all, just look it up real quick. Doesn't require downloads or anything.   
After I did that, and lowered my 1440p to 1080p, the game ran mostly smooth (but not perfect). 
Hopefully this helps others to enjoy this amazing game, and hopefully Townfall will be better optimized.
The upscaling and overall it's a bit lacking for the current year, but it's still a great game!

Recommended 100%
Finished all the different possible endings, collect all the memos and pictures. 
This game at the starts off really good, but as it progresses you question what exactly are you playing.
All in all, a solid 8/10.

I tried so hard to get into this game. And it really wasn't terrible for me, I think I just couldn't find the same magic that so many other people got from this one. I never played it back in the day, so I don't have the same nostalgia that a lot of people do for it. I don't have much to objectively criticize, so I feel like it's unfair to give it a negative review (pretty neutral for me). It just started to get a bit tedious with a lot of backtracking for puzzles after about 10 hours.

There's a reason the franchise and more specifically Silent Hill 2 is as popular as it is, everything about it is a masterpiece. 

However there does come a time in every survival horror puzzle game (not just this one) where the frustration begins to outweigh the creepiness, but maybe that's my own temperament and no issue of the game itself (although the length of this game is almost double that of the originals so that may play a factor) 

My only real complaint is that it is quite jittery even with a relatively high-end pc. I had to take a couple months break from going for the full 100% on this game as it's frustrating and takes away from the experience quite a bit. But its nothing a couple performance mods or setting tweaks cant fix im sure.
7/10 i like the thrilling and mysterious Story But Stil Playing the game felt so aboring since im LITRALLY doing the same thing but in different map

I played Silent Hill 2 after Silent Hill f. I have to admit Silent Hill 2 is unnerving and never stops bringing new surprises. Sounds in the game are insanely good. The story is peak silent hill. 10/10

all I can say is that i love this game too much. I grew up on silent hill lore so i was really excited on the remake. Thank you bloober team for staying true to the original. I can't wait for Silent Hill 1 Remake.

Recommended. I enjoyed it. Well aware of the criticism that this is a demake, worse than the original. I respect those opinions but I don't agree. The original might have given you nightmares in the 2000s in the dark behind a CRT, but it can't achieve the same today. Loved the acting. Blooper did a nice job on some areas. The cut content is a little shame. Boss fights are a lot better. Some scenes are a step down (eg a certain chase scene), but most are not. The psychological horror is still prominently present.

Fair criticism:
- Lack of difficulty, on normal the game is ridiculously easy. Allows stockpiling of resources. The dodge is completely overpowered. Combine the two and failure becomes unlikely. Resources seem to have their location fixed. Adapting to the player (eg Resident Evil) would have made the experience better and scarier.
- Heavy shader stuttering. Modders fixed it, but it should have been fixed by Blooper. 30fps cutscenes feel out of place.
- Reusing puzzle/environmental elements too often. Especially the valve, the breakable wall, movable cart, etc. The lack of variety becomes noticeable.
- Weird video game logic. Pretty sure that lock can be opened with a bolt cutter. The weirdest one (spoiler): [spoiler]No paint can requires a can opener, the metal shavings would contaminate the paint.[/spoiler].
- Lack of enemy variety. Although faithful to the original, this one had room for improvement.

first time playing a silent hill game and is pretty good story wise and gameplay too the combat is a bit weak but still good i see people saying there is too much walking but is alright is just if you want to get everything and i did and it was rerwarding in my opinion, only bad thing i can say the performance is really bad because is UE5.
Overall pretty good!

I'll start off by saying that I haven't played the original but this remake really puts into focus why silent hill 2 was such a definitive surival horror masterpiece

Atmospheric, oppressive and straight up brutal at time. James' trip to Silent Hill is just captivating, learning everything about him and people around him (even if you know the story already) is so engaging and the gameplay while simple serves it's purpose with great level design to boot.

Only small problem is performance, this remake is not super optimized and running it on lower specs I guess could be a bit of an headache. So keep that in mind
Absolutely superb experience. A must play for any horror / single player lover.
What a game. The type of story that sticks with you for a long while. Must scavenge more desk drawers...

first thing first: this is not a carbon copy of the original. It is a creative revisioning of a classic.
Sections have been expanded and the game length padded, as well as an action combat system introduced. I was sceptical, too, when I saw the first trailers, which had a focus on the action combat and in my first playhtrough I hated the excessive Mannequin placement in every section, instead of a few smart ones... but once you get to New Game+ and find the "secret" melee weapon, it gets very easy.
Some might not like the padded playtime, but Bloober team did an outstanding job capturing the mood of the original with the new graphics and it is just nice looking at the environment again and again.
A pity also they got rid of the Twin-Peaks inspired "acting" of the characters. They decided to take a more dramatic and serious approach, which also works well in this case.
The only downside is the missed opportunity to use the fog to cover areas, so your machine doesn't have to render it (yes, landmarks are rendering behind the fog, even if you don't see them), causing frame rate drops and high usage of the GPU.

I still love this game despite its flaws. It's my favorite remake, which allows the charm of the original to stand out even more.

one of the most bizarre psychological horror game i have played it has such a deep storyline with a lot to try and understand this game is really good the soundtrack of this game really makes the horror of this game and the environment really also makes this game scary it just makes you feel uncomfortable so this game one of the top horror games for me just the performance isnt that great but still playable most of the time

it good, it scary, and made me feel fog... 9/10 game only cuz that labyrinth level because that was boring ahhh... and it dragged a lil in the end but overall pretty good... did not expect the ending :0

(went in playing this fully blind) 
very well made remake. a truly horrific experience without the use of cliche jumpscares. most of the horror comes from the atmosphere which is just insanely uncomfortable at times. both the visuals and sound effects make sure u never know whats going to actually happen and always keep u on edge. 

combat and most of the enemy variety in the game is pretty limited, however for the length of the game and the vibe they were going for its fine to get through with. the bosses are all unique at least.

its a very faithful remake to the original and u can tell they just improved on the original and added more to the game. the story is intriguing enough to keep you guessing and wanting to know more through the entire game. at some point the game really just turns into melancholy simulator. 

overall it feels a lot like playing newer RE games however less goofy and with less explosions but way more terror, horrifying visuals and a mindwrecking vibe that truly resembles a nightmare. highly recommended if u want something tense.

This game is masterpiece, must play for everyone. Every aspect of the game is done on highest level possible, especially the atmosphere and plot. i advice you to ready every newspaper and note you find in this game, it really worth it and there not so many to tire you.

Psychological and emotional masterpiece. This game has an atmosphere that seeps deep into your psyche, from focusing on dark themes of raw human degeneracy and moral conflict to the manifestation of a tailor made level of hell for the main protagonist James. Its incredible and horrifying, The type of experience that stays with you long after you've put the game down. All the polaroid photos combine and decoded read as "you've been here for two decades" also the tv static has morse code 10/10

I am a Silent Hill purist. I played SH1 on a demo disc back in the PS days, and was so intrigued had to buy it. My teenager brain didnt understand what exactly I was getting into. A journey that led me down symbolism, alchemy and Jungian psychology. 

Anyway, Silent Hill 2 remake. Its great. Silent Hill 2, the original, was an incredibly special game to me. I feared that, they would ruin the remake. I havent finished it yet, about halfway thru, and other Silent Hill purists should enjoy this.

The fighting is done just right. They've managed to retain the fear of janky combat from back in the day while keeping it slick and modern.

Just do it. Maybe when its on sale (games are way too overpriced these days) but do it. 

Linux users: if you are on nividia, use the following:

DXVK_FILTER_DEVICE_NAME=2080 PROTON_DLSS_UPGRADE=1 PROTON_USE_NTSYNC=1 gamemoderun %command% 

game runs like TRASH out of the box, but a tweak and its fine.

story is fantastic and the design of this game is beautiful. While its not the scariest game ever to exist the atmosphere is great. The pacing is good and in my personal opinion it only struggles at the prison and labyrinth cube puzzle thing. Combat is simplistic but fun, and the puzzles are all pretty good besides a few like the gallows. other than that this game is a must play, honestly it goes in my top 3 games OAT and is a must play for anyone into psychological horror

UEVR that's all it.  Do it properly and play the game from the inside.  You can't say you love psychological horror unless you spend some time actually in Silent Hill hearing the sirens blare over the groans of the unspeakable horrors coming for you in the fog.  Running through an abandoned hospital watching it deteriorate into a nightmarish hellscape of rusty wire floors and jagged concrete as you run from the faceless nurses that come to tear you apart.  Play this in VR...

I’ve been a fan of Silent Hill 2 since the original release 20+ years ago and I already finished the remake on PS5 on a TV. But trying it in VR through UEVR honestly feels like experiencing the game properly for the first time.

This game was made for VR. I just slowly walk around enjoying the atmosphere, locations, lighting and all the tiny details you barely notice on a flat screen. Silent Hill in VR feels like a real place instead of just a game.

At this point I barely even play flat-screen games anymore unless they have VR support or a VR mod. Developers seriously need to stop ignoring VR already. VR modders are the only reason I can truly enjoy games like this today.
Honestly my introduction into silent hill and I'm very impressed at how the game drew me into the eerie world built around James.

this game is a great FPS and story it depth of story telling is immense. With  the plot of James going through the stages of grief you just get so enveloped in the search for redemption that James wants it is a epic story 9/10.

Güzel bir remake olduğunu düşünüyorum. Orijinal oyuna %100 sadık kalmasa da farklı hissettirmiyor, mekanlar vb. Geliştirilmiş ve üstüne eklenmiş tıpa tıp aynı değil. Oyunun korku dengesini bozmamışlar ve uzatmamışlar bence tam olması gerektiği gibi bir Remake.

One of the greatest horror games ive ever played. 10/10 masterpiece . the story of this game is so deep. While you might not understand it fully first time beating it , its one of those games that may require some to play it again and or youtube a video of greater in depth explanation. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED
i deadass will either be drunk or high and just walk around the start area and look around it looks that good.

Silent Hill 2 was always the least scary out of the original four games in my opinion.
That being said this is the scariest game I've ever played in my entire life.
it's basically the same game, but, what was changed was an IMPROVEMENT. 
I have faith that the Silent Hill 1 remake will be excellent! Bloober team really did the fans a service with this one! you can genuinely tell this was a labor of love.


Edit: oh yeah and it also runs flawlessly on Linux.
Amazing game! but prolly get it on discount, I don't think any game is worth 100 bucks

Words can't quite describe how phenomenal this game is. One of the most faithful remakes I have seen that has maintained its quality that the OG had. I really have very little to say about it that isn't just that you have to experience if for yourself. Long time fan or new to the franchise, I firmly believe everyone can love it. The actors' performances are incredible, the ambiance is there, the combat is refreshing. This is THE comeback that put Silent Hill back on the map, and I couldn't be happier that it was thanks to Bloober's work. Even after going for all the endings and achievements, a year later I still yearn to go back to Silent Hill 2. A brand new classic that I can't wait to replay.

A good story, but very poor optimization (the developers don't know how to use UE5), very poor animations, boring, repetitive locations (it's really not interesting to explore the same location multiple times, just with different textures), cringe jump scares with very loud sounds. I wouldn't recommend playing this, but you should watch it somewhere, as the story is quite well-developed.
It's like if Resident Evil put less emphasis on combat and more on story, puzzles and world building.  How you play the game will affect the outcome, so make your choices carefully.
An absolutely stunning remake of one of the best psychological horror games of all time. Buy it.

Such a beautiful yet terrifying experience all in one game. The HDR and raytracing bring the beauty to silent hill inside of its very one one of a kind terrifying atmosphere. A very well remake of one of the best horror games of all time. The added content from team Bloober actually fit the game very well. The game doesn't seem to have the best optimization but can't say I've really ran into to many issues. Turn off the lights, crank up your headset and enjoy the Silent Hill experience. Maybe if your lucky Pyramid Head will come looking for you.

The game is simply way too long and bloated in the worst way possible. It's twice the length of the original and the way they achieved this was by adding more complex puzzles and confusing maze-like levels. It's easy to get lost and you have to look at the map every single minute or so. The game tells you at the end how many times you've opened the map and the average number posted online is around 1000, with some reaching over 2000... in a 20 hour long game. 

The bloated length completely kills the horror in my opinion. I'm no longer scared when I'm forced to run around looking at a map every single minute trying to figure out where the game wants me to go next. I suspect this was done simply to justify the high price of the game. A 10 hour long game wouldn't sell for 80 dollars. If you enjoy looking at maps and solving puzzles with the occasional combat fighting weird fleshy monsters this game might be for you.
Awesome areas, gameplay, story, atmosphere and story

Those weird puzzles and places that are falling apart are luckily explained at the end. Combat and survival is great.
My first game where even with a bunch of guns i felt like a victim 10/10
It could definitely use some optimization but it got me tearing up towards the end, highly recommend.

Performance actually terrible. Very bad PC port. Game overstayed its welcome. PUZZLE SIMULATOR. Find a key to a open door, find puzzle piece to find code to unlock puzzle, then to acquire another key to unlock a door to EVEN MORE PUZZLES. 

Combat is simple, a bit janky when it comes to melee, but overall competent enough .

The main draw to this game is its story and atmosphere.  Its probably the best in its genre. To the haunting ambient noise, to the eerie music echoing through dim lit corridors. Its honestly fantastic. 

Story is great albeit a little vague at times for good reason. Its very fun to unravel the mystery behind silent hill and the unfortunate characters that roam it.

Would recommend on sale

An amazing game with a very interesting story.
Combat and fear factor are top of the line.

I wish for ports of the originals
I really love the original version and I could type a lot about how I think a lot of changes are downgrades like the pyramid head chase scene with maria or reuniting with maria but there are a lot of upgrades like eddie and angela to make up for it so its still one of the best games ever

Silent Hill 2 User Manual

- Get a tetanus shot

- Listen to Lofi Girl for 1 hour after every gaming session

- Have tissues on hand

- And find the dog's key (Canon)
Amazing music, made me jump 5 centimeters off my chair and overall a wild spooky experience

I had heard a lot about this game and people saying it's very good.
Even though I have not played the original and didn't know much about the story, I decided to play it cause I adore survival horror games.
I must say it did not dissapoint and it was beyond words can describe.
The graphics are incredible, the whole atmosphere is eerily beautiful as well as the whole story: moving throughout Silent Hill alongside James, experiencing his emotional weight and guiding him to accept the truth - or even choose to deny it.
I think this game is one of those games that really linger and stay with you after you've played them.
I adored it and would absolutely play it again.
The best horror game of all time, sad story, a game that you won't forget!!

If you run the game in dx11 and disable nanites this game becomes a lot easier to run. I can consistently get 60fps which is pretty horrid to say but it's well worth suffering through the 60fps and even 30 fps just to play the game. By far one of my favorite games and one of the coolest speedruns I have ever experienced. Everyone should play this game at least once.

This game has some great moment moments and great interesting story that make it worth a play. its about 6 hours to long and the second half dragged waaaaaaaaaaay to hard with not much plot progression. I don't really understand the glaze and i'm feeling a 7/10.
Had me ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ myself many times, some of the best ambience in a horror game ever. 10/10 depression simulator. Can be a resource hog of a game though. Especially at higher resolutions.
Been so stressed at work and needed to refresh my mind. Idk why I ended up buying and playing this game. But it makes me feel something, tho. Recommended

Before you ask....
Yes if you ask me what was my remake of the year for 2024 this will be the one.
Yes if i have to make top 3 games of 2024,it will be there.
Yes,i have played the original game twice with all of the endings unlocked on that version,i played on a pcsx2 emulator a few years back in high school,and another time on the pc enached edition on pc(a version made by fans that fixes the pc port of the original game,if you do want to give the original silent hill 2 a shot,this is your best bet as of now) and yes i think that with how much silent hill 2 is a masterpiece,i do like the remake a bit more....

Silent hill 2 was never just another video game,it was an inspired work of art and it will dive very deep into psychological horror,the remake plays into that a lot more so go in expacting that in mind.

A great example of this can be seen in the second section of the first "dungeon" of the game,you have a part of a big puzzle you have to complete surronding a clock in a safe area and you have to go to an certain apartment to get another key of the puzzle to continue,you go to that apartment and everything is fine,even more than fine regular 90s apartment regular shining and clean(a modarate spolier but a far cry from the rest of the area) you go to the bathroom and pick the item key you need....
Than everything slams and shifts completely,you get you from the bathroom but not to the same apartment you entered to,everything is dark,rotten and melts like no one touched it in years,the layout and design of the apartment is changed entirely,and now you have to manover just to get out of the place compared to how easy it was to get in,it is in those small moments even in like the first two hours of the game that it knows how to mess around with you something the original kept only to a much more late game area.

Another example can be found in the hotel the final "dungeon"of sorts in the game,at first the hotel is stunning when you enter it and in first scout,but the more you actually explore the hotel,it starts to deteriorate and get the true form of it,old,soked and rusty is starts with the little of details and then it slowly starts to build up the more you progress the hotel and it's enviorments,by the time you are about to go to the objective in the form of room 312 the entire hotel and the lobby in spesifics is in a rough spot,the place looks abandoned,shattered glass from the windows on the floor already starting to tease the otherworld transform of the hotel that comes after room 312 and it's done masterfully,the otherworld transformation is terrifying,if the hotels true form looks like an abandoned ressort than the otherworld hotel looks like the entire building was a crashsite from 5 dacades ago(to compare,in the original game the hotel looks exactly the same until the events of room 312 occur just to turn into the otherworld version)the remake took its time to build up the transformation with the expended time it has to truly give this title the love and respect it deserves,it is shown in both examples and it is shown that they are using the extended time they have to play more with the psychological horror setting (and also to extend playtime).

Characters are mostly an improvment from the original,mainly because we spent a bit more time with them,most of the delivary of the lines is great(besides one scene in the hospital where thr original clearly takes it when you reunite with a certain character),it is also where the extanded game run time checks in again to give us more time with those people like instead of the bowl o rama scene which was mostly a comidic scene in the original game you get a much more dark scene with eddie sitting in the dark eating ice cream and with where that Conversation goes with him it is a great way to tease what will come when we meet him next,the same goes to angela,after you both meet in the apartment it take a very long time to meet her again(you dont meet her again up until the labyrinth,in the reamake they added more scenes with her like the one at night after the hospital where it was also placed to progress her character arc and to shed more light about her as a person,the remake takes the extended run time it has to flash out the characters more and to give them a much more builded up story arc which is a nice thing to see them do that also besides just focusing on expanding the gameplay parts,i don't find myself having a problem with some of the redesigns of the characters,maria's redesign is the least offending of the two cases we have,her design changed to appeal to what is more...."attracting" nowdays was it necessary???,not really but you can get why they tried to do with it,the sexual appeal of the design is a key part of the theme of the character,angela face redesign is noticible at like one frame at the graveyard,in the rest of the game she feels fine,overall i really like what they did with the remakes take on the cast,they kept what worked,redid what needed a redo and builded up and expended the arc of the characters even with some small scenes here and there.

Gameplay wise,it is clunky but it is also kind of the point,james is a regular guy and on top of that not in the best of shape,not physically nor mentally,attacks drag out,you have to time them in order not to get needlessly hurt,you also have a dodge botton but it is a bit slaggish so you have to time it,it not really a point of critic,quite the opposite it works in the games favor, a lot of games that came out after took notes and also managed to do so quite well(re7,amnesia the bunker and conscript are all great examples of this),but it is also a matter of taste if you don't have the patience for it,don't even bother,just know what you are getting yourself into before you rush to critic this.

Boss fights are not bullets sponges anymore,more than that they are actually solid,they are also get better in an sort of an accelerated way,you can feel the tesion burning in the eddie boss fight for example somthing i could not bring myself to say about the original.

The music is somthing that feels like a dream,there is no other way to explain it,akira yamaoka slaps everytime he touches an instrument for this IP and it's not even funny,yes it took me sometime to get used to the new tracks but they grew on me they are great some of them stand tall with the original soundtrack,i still listen to all of them or just go back to them,they have a certian vibe in the music and the remake manages to get there most of the time.

Silent hill itself is just as much as a character as the rest of the cast,the place feels alive even tho it is desserted and full of fog and monsters it has places to explore,lore to read about the town and it's history,the whole thing with the cult from silent hill 1 and 3 is also in the backround but the mystery is about the town itself and how all of this manages to happen in reality nontheless,ever since the seventh generation rolled around,you could have only imagined what an explorible silent hill would be like(i personally always loved exploraition,it is my favorite part of like every game)and now i finally have it,and i loved it espically with the atmosphere and vibes.

A warning,this story is heavy and extremely dark,it talk frequently about serious topics,but it's all worth it in the end,the story is basically the reason you are even here,a plot so deep it has multiple layers to it which leads it all to be left to the player interpatation,to this day i amazed how well written it all is and it all comes togather to make a truly timeless story with all of those themes and allegories and the topics the story covers,for me it is perfect.

Overall even with some perfomence issue and even if it can't truly replace the original game,the remake vastly improved on silent hill 2 and i can't recommend it enough

10/10
Leave is canon
Leave me alone.
Pretty true to the original. Puzzles were... good, not great like the OG. Still, an amazing remake and definitely worth playing whether you are an old fan or new of the Silent Hill franchise.

Even though I never played the original, I can understand why this part of the series is highly praised and considered the greatest psychological horror story of all time. The atmosphere is just incredible, and the sounds makes you feel like a constant sense of dread that gets under your skin. Good sound and the environment makes the game more horrifying than just relying on constant jump scares. I loved exploring the town and the characters and piecing together what was happening.  The fact that after finishing the game caused me to search up Youtube videos or posts to learn about the symbolism of every aspect of the game, and theories shows just how impactful the story is. Seriously, this is probably the game that made psychological horror so loved and popular. The only downside is that the combat isn't great (but then again, combat is not the main focus of the game), and this game is HEAVY (Unreal Engine of course), and will suffer if you don't have a strong PC (using a ASUS Rog Ally, and yeah, playing it can be rough sometimes). Can't wait for the remake for the first Silent hill game, and very excited for Townfall. Also, will def try out Silent Hill F!

At first glance, the game seemed boring to me, but after some time I finally decided to play through it. It has the best atmosphere I’ve ever experienced in a game. The soundtrack gives me chills, and the fog-covered town during the first playthrough is absolutely mesmerizing.
The enemies are unlike anything I had ever seen in other games — you never really know what to expect from them. Every possible moment in the game is carefully thought out down to the smallest detail, and almost every scene has an explanation that you only discover during a second playthrough.
The number of endings is also impressive, and they make you want to replay the game again and again.

I like the atmosphere in this game. This game represents Melancholy, loneliness, liminal feeling, dreamlike atmosphere, and Psychological weight. These are what I want to feel when I play Horror games.

A journey into a horrifying city, where you will feel isolated and lost. It's an experience.

P.S. Turn off the lights before playing it and wear a headset.

Playing this game with my BEAUTIFUL girlfriend was one of the best experiences of my life. This game is a masterpiece of horror. It is thought provoking, disturbing, and atmospheric. My girlfriend and I had the best time playing this together and solving puzzles together and finally beating the game together. If you're thinking of buying this game, do not hesitate.
